creator: karl@pielorz.com
text:
(In reply to kpielorz from comment #7)

Forgot to say - Linux and Windows on the same XenServers don't seem to have any issue with the clock skewing ahead (or at least they don't have an issue that you can 'see'). e.g. Linux drops a second or so on a migrate - but NTP brings it back into line.

Windows doesn't seem to gain or lose anything (I suspect XenServer Tools are re-setting the clock at the end of the migrate, as Windows NTP isn't a continuous adjustment NTP like ntpd on Linux/FreeBSD).
